inclusions.description: Unrefined copper; copper anodes for electrolytic refining

Refined copper and copper alloys, unwrought. Note 1(a): 'Refined copper' = (i) ≥99.85% Cu by weight; OR (ii) ≥97.5% Cu by weight with all other elements below specified table limits. Note 1(b): 'Copper alloys' = metallic substances (other than unrefined copper) where Cu predominates over each other element AND either: (i) at least one element exceeds its table limit; or (ii) total other elements >2.5% by weight. Note 1(c): 'Master alloys' = contain >10% Cu, not usefully malleable, used as additives/de-oxidants in non-ferrous metallurgy — EXCEPTION: copper phosphide with >15% P → heading 28.53
Master alloys of copper (Note 1(c): >10% Cu, not malleable, used as additive/de-oxidant in non-ferrous metallurgy)
Copper powders and flakes: (a) powders of non-lamellar structure; (b) powders of lamellar structure (flakes) and flakes
Copper plates, sheets and strip, of a thickness exceeding 0.15 mm
Copper foil (whether or not printed, or backed with paper, paperboard, plastics or similar backing materials) of a thickness (excluding any backing) not exceeding 0.15 mm
Copper tube or pipe fittings (e.g., couplings, elbows, sleeves)
Stranded wire, cables, plaited bands and the like, of copper, not electrically insulated
[Legacy — cloth, grill and netting of copper wire; now largely subsumed into 74.19]

Nails, tacks, drawing pins, staples and similar articles, of copper or with shafts of iron or steel and heads of copper; screws, bolts, nuts, screw hooks, rivets, cotters, cotter-pins, washers and similar articles, of copper
[Legacy — cooking or heating apparatus of a non-domestic type of copper]
Table, kitchen or other household articles and parts thereof, of copper; pot scourers and scouring or polishing pads, gloves and the like, of copper; sanitary ware and parts thereof, of copper
Other articles of copper: cloth, grill, netting; expanded metal; chain and parts thereof; other articles

exclusions.condition: Copper phosphide (phosphor copper) containing more than 15% by weight of phosphorus
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 28.53 (inorganic compounds NES) — Note 1(c) explicit exception. Copper phosphide with ≤15% P qualifies as a master alloy → 74.05. With >15% P → 28.53

exclusions.condition: Copper compounds (copper oxide, copper sulphate, copper chloride, copper carbonate, verdigris, etc.)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 28 (inorganic) or Chapter 29 (organic copper compounds). Metallic copper → Ch.74; chemical compounds → Ch.28/29

exclusions.condition: Electrically insulated copper wire or cable
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 85.44 (insulated wire, cable and other insulated electric conductors). Bare copper wire → 74.08. Stranded bare copper cable → 74.13. Insulated → 85.44
Redirect to heading 94.06 (prefabricated buildings). Copper structural elements prepared for use in buildings → 74.19 or 74.07. Complete prefabricated building of copper → 94.06

classification_rules.rule: Note 1 — Three-tier definition: refined copper vs copper alloy vs master alloy
classification_rules.description: Three distinct categories in Ch.74: (1) 'Refined copper' (74.03): ≥99.85% Cu; OR ≥97.5% Cu with all individual impurities below table limits. (2) 'Copper alloys' (74.03 alloy subheadings): Cu predominates over each other element AND either one element exceeds its limit or total others >2.5%. Common alloys: brass (Cu-Zn), bronze (Cu-Sn), cupronickel (Cu-Ni), cupro-silicon, beryllium copper. (3) 'Master alloys' (74.05): >10% Cu, NOT malleable, used as metallurgical additive/de-oxidant. Master alloys are not usable as structural materials; they are consumed in manufacturing other alloys. Exception: copper phosphide >15% P → 28.53, not 74.05.

classification_rules.rule: Subheading Note — Brasses (Cu-Zn), bronzes (Cu-Sn) and other copper alloy types
classification_rules.description: Subheading Note 1 defines specific copper alloy families for subheading classification within 74.03: (a) Copper-zinc base alloys (BRASSES) — Cu-Zn with or without other elements, zinc being the predominant added element. (b) Copper-tin base alloys (BRONZES) — Cu-Sn with or without other elements. (c) Copper-nickel alloys (cupro-nickel/nickel silver) — Cu-Ni ± Zn. (d) Copper-nickel-zinc alloys (nickel silver). These definitions drive subheading splits within heading 74.03 and similarly across 74.07–74.19.

Primary copper classification path: 74.01 = copper MATTES (pyrometallurgical intermediate, sulphide-based) and cement copper (precipitated, impure). 74.02 = UNREFINED copper (blister copper) and copper anodes for electrolytic refining — not yet meeting 'refined' purity. 74.03 = REFINED copper (≥99.85% or ≥97.5% meeting limits) and copper ALLOYS — final unwrought metal forms (ingots, billets, cathodes, wire bars). 74.04 = copper WASTE AND SCRAP. The metallurgical purity and processing stage determines which heading applies.

Thickness of 0.15 mm is the dividing line for flat copper products (NOTE: 0.15 mm for copper vs 0.20 mm for aluminium (76.06/76.07) vs 0.20 mm for tin (80.04/80.05)). > 0.15 mm → 74.09 (plates, sheets and strip). ≤ 0.15 mm → 74.10 (foil). Foil may be printed or backed with paper/plastics without changing classification; only the copper thickness (excluding backing) determines the heading.

Three headings for elongated copper products: 74.07 = bars, rods and profiles (solid, larger cross-section; profiles have non-circular/non-rectangular cross-sections). 74.08 = wire (cross-section fits within circle <6 mm diameter per Section XV Note 9(c) — single solid or stranded as wire). 74.13 = STRANDED wire, cables, plaited bands (multiple wires twisted/stranded together, bare/uninsulated). Insulated wire/cable of any type → 85.44.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 74.18 (household copper articles) vs 74.19 (other copper articles) vs Ch.82/83 (tools/hardware)
classification_rules.description: 74.18 = table/kitchen/household copper articles and sanitary ware (copper pots, pans, faucets, bathroom fixtures). 74.19 = all other copper articles (copper mesh, chain, fasteners not in 74.15, expanded metal, general articles). When a copper article has a specific heading in Ch.82 (copper tools), Ch.83 (copper padlocks, fittings, miscellaneous) or Ch.84+ (machinery), those functional headings take priority. Copper padlock → 83.01. Copper plumbing tap/valve → 84.81. Copper cooking pot → 74.18.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 74.15 (copper fasteners) vs 73.17/73.18 (steel fasteners) — material determines heading
classification_rules.description: Heading 74.15 covers fasteners OF copper or WITH copper heads + iron/steel shafts: nails, tacks, drawing pins, staples, screws, bolts, nuts, rivets, washers. Pure copper fasteners → 74.15. Steel/iron fasteners with copper head → 74.15. Steel/iron fasteners without copper components → 73.17 (nails/tacks) or 73.18 (screws/bolts/nuts). The material of the fastener (or its head) determines the chapter.
